class FenwickTree
  def initialize(size)
    @n = size
    @tree = Array.new(@n + 1, 0)
  end

  def update(index, delta)
    while index <= @n
      @tree[index] += delta
      index += index & -index
    end
  end

  def query(index)
    sum = 0
    while index > 0
      sum += @tree[index]
      index -= index & -index
    end
    sum
  end
end

def solve(nums, queries)
  def compute_depth(x)
    if x == 1
      return 0
    end
    depth = 0
    current = x
    while current != 1
      current = current.to_s(2).count('1')
      depth += 1
      if current == 1
        break
      end
    end
    return depth
  end

  n = nums.length
  ft = Array.new(6) { FenwickTree.new(n) }

  depths = nums.map { |x| compute_depth(x) }

  depths.each_with_index do |d, i|
    ft[d].update(i + 1, 1)
  end

  answer = []
  queries.each do |q|
    if q[0] == 1
      l = q[1]
      r = q[2]
      k = q[3]
      sum = ft[k].query(r + 1) - ft[k].query(l)
      answer << sum
    else
      idx = q[1]
      val = q[2]
      old_val = nums[idx]
      old_depth = depths[idx]
      ft[old_depth].update(idx + 1, -1)
      new_depth = compute_depth(val)
      ft[new_depth].update(idx + 1, 1)
      nums[idx] = val
      depths[idx] = new_depth
    end
  end

  answer
end

class FenwickTree
  def initialize(size)
    @n = size
    @tree = Array.new(@n + 1, 0)
  end

  def update(index, delta)
    while index <= @n
      @tree[index] += delta
      index += index & -index
    end
  end

  def query(index)
    sum = 0
    while index > 0
      sum += @tree[index]
      index -= index & -index
    end
    sum
  end
end

def solve(nums, queries)
  def compute_depth(x)
    if x == 1
      return 0
    end
    depth = 0
    current = x
    while current != 1
      current = current.to_s(2).count('1')
      depth += 1
      if current == 1
        break
      end
    end
    return depth
  end

  n = nums.length
  ft = Array.new(6) { FenwickTree.new(n) }

  depths = nums.map { |x| compute_depth(x) }

  depths.each_with_index do |d, i|
    ft[d].update(i + 1, 1)
  end

  answer = []
  queries.each do |q|
    if q[0] == 1
      l = q[1]
      r = q[2]
      k = q[3]
      sum = ft[k].query(r + 1) - ft[k].query(l)
      answer << sum

      ft[new_depth].update(idx + 1, 1)
      nums[idx] = val
      depths[idx] = new_depth
    end
  end

  answer
end

    while index <= @n
      @tree[index] += delta
      index += index & -index
    end

def solve(nums, queries)
  def compute_depth(x)

class FenwickTree
  def initialize(size)
    @n = size
    @tree = Array.new(@n + 1, 0)
  end

  def update(index, delta)
    while index <= @n
      @tree[index] += delta
      index += index & -index
    end
  end

  def query(index)
    sum = 0
    while index > 0
      sum += @tree[index]
      index -= index & -index
    end
    sum
  end
end

def solve(nums, queries)
  def compute_depth(x)
    if x == 1
      return 0
    end
    depth = 0
    current = x
    while current != 1
      current = current.to_s(2).count('1')
      depth += 1
      if current == 1
        break
      end
    end
    return depth
  end

  n = nums.length
  ft = Array.new(6) { FenwickTree.new(n) }

  depths = nums.map { |x| compute_depth(x) }

  depths.each_with_index do |d, i|
    ft[d].update(i + 1, 1)
  end

  answer = []
  queries.each do |q|
    if q[0] == 1
      l = q[1]

      k = q[3]

      depths[idx] = new_depth
    end
  end

  answer
end

class FenwickTree
  def initialize(size)
    @n = size
    @tree = Array.new(@n + 1, 0)
  end

  def update(index, delta)

      @tree[index] += delta
      index += index & -index
    end
  end

    sum = 0
    while index > 0
      sum += @tree[index]
      index -= index & -index
    end
    sum
  end
end

      nums[idx] = val
      depths[idx] = new_depth
    end
  end

  answer
end
